# S3 classes

::: callout-tip
## Objective

Give our list of pointers an S3 class so R will print it nicely.

:::

We have a `format_*` function, but R doesn't know to *use* it. When you type the
name of an object, R dispatches to a `print`/`format` method based on the
object's **class**. So we need to give our list a class.

## Classes are the whole hook

R's S3 system is driven entirely by the `class` attribute. If an object has class
`"foo"`, then calling a generic like `format()` on it looks for `format.foo()`.
That's it—attach a class, define a method, and you plug straight into R.

## The `vctrs_vctr` class

The [vctrs](https://vctrs.r-lib.org/) package defines a very general
`vctrs_vctr` class that gives sensible default behavior for vector-like objects.
We'll include it in our class vector so we get that behavior for free, ending
with the underlying `list` type:

```r
c("myclass", "vctrs_vctr", "list")
```

## Setting the class from Rust

We can assign a class to a **mutable** `Robj` with `.set_class()`. Because
`set_class()` is fallible, the function returns a `Result`.

## Example

```rust
#[extendr]
fn parse_shapes(x: Strings) -> extendr_api::Result<List> {
    let mut res = x
        .into_iter()
        .map(|xi| match Shape::new(xi) {
            Ok(shape) => shape.into_robj(),
            Err(_) => ().into_robj(),
        })
        .collect::<List>();

    res.set_class(&["shapes", "vctrs_vctr", "list"])?;
    Ok(res)
}
```

## The R method

The class is only half the story—now define the method on the R side. In an R
script (e.g. `R/rurls.R`), define `format.rurls` and have it call your Rust
helper:

```r
#' @export
format.rurls <- function(x, ...) {
    format_rurls(x)
}
```

## Exercise

- Update `url_parse()` to return `extendr_api::Result<List>`
- Set the class to `c("rurls", "vctrs_vctr", "list")` on the result
- Add the `format.rurls` method above in an R script
- `devtools::document()` and `devtools::load_all()`, then print a parsed vector

<details>
    <summary>View solution</summary>

```rust
#[extendr]
fn url_parse(x: Strings) -> extendr_api::Result<List> {
    let mut res = x
        .into_iter()
        .map(|xi| {
            if xi.is_na() {
                return ().into_robj();
            }

            match RUrl::new(xi) {
                Ok(v) => v.into_robj(),
                Err(_) => ().into_robj(),
            }
        })
        .collect::<List>();

    res.set_class(&["rurls", "vctrs_vctr", "list"])?;
    Ok(res)
}
```

```r
#' @export
format.rurls <- function(x, ...) {
    format_rurls(x)
}
```

</details>
